item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*plushie shark friends swim off*

:D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*perches whale shark plushie on head, upvotes*

*holds up bamboo shark plushie*

*insists that the two be friends*

I actually have a plushie bamboo shark that I bought from an aquarium because it was so soft and fuzzy and had little fang-whisker things and everything, plus I could use it as a blindfold when my college roommate was staying up late working on essays and the light in the dorm room was hard to sleep through

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*perches whale shark plushie on head, upvotes*

Whale sharks are best sharks! Also, I really like the dreamtime. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bit of a necropost, but I was wondering; were you were thinking of the kangaroo trickster from The Last Continent when you wrote Nikolai's dialogue?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Selachiosk Pungix Combin. SPC. Fuck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pratchett. I attribute it all to a steady diet of Terry Pratchett, because no one does silly better than Pratchett, and no one does serious better than Pratchett, and definitely no one does both at the same time better than Pratchett.

On the other hand, you let those madd writing skillz get to your head and (on occasion) behave as though your interpretation is the only one (I could be reading you wrong, though, and if this is the case, I apologize).

This, on the other hand, is something that would be much better brought up with me personally, rather than in a discussion thread for a scip. Not like it's hard to PM me or something, I'm in chat constantly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Djoric, I can't make up my damn mind about you. On the one hand, you are an objectively excellent writer, and can turn the SPC into something hilarious, serious, and intelligent at the same time without killing the silly, pre-existing shit. You can write stories like "Mary-Ann and Salah" like a boss, create/expand mythos with stuff like this and "Between Two Trees," and overall just stun your audience in a good way. On the other hand, you let those madd writing skillz get to your head and (on occasion) behave as though your interpretation is the only one (I could be reading you wrong, though, and if this is the case, I apologize). I know I don't know you, and I know you don't know me, and I know I'm new here, but Djoric, you magnificent bastard, you're fucking my whole understanding of literature up. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Right. Shark Punching Center. Doesn't matter how many people hate it, it will always find its way in under the cracks. Loved this. Loved the tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The best thing is that everything he says is either a literal description of something or a trickily-hidden metaphor/reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This. While in the dream, you have the exact same powers of alteration as you do in real life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It doesn't translate well into English, and it doesn't help that every tribe has a slightly different spin on the mythos/philosophy.

For the purposes of this article, think of the Dreamtime like an Astral Plane or spirit world. Another level of reality that people usually only get to while dreaming. That's not 100% accurate, but it will suffice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aboriginal Australian mythos as to the creation of the world - before the World, there was the Dreamtime. Beyond that I don't know much more

I could even be wrong about this…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I appear to be the only person on the site who has not heard of any "dreamtime" before. Nonetheless, hearty upvote to you. That's excellent word salad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is 2D mode still a top-down picture if it swims up onto a wall? Or does it shift to a side view?

Why does it need the suspended, no-touch containment tank? Can it shift to the other side of a surface it has "projected" itself on to? If not, I think we only need to make sure that the seams on the module are smaller than 1449's least 2D width.

From the rabbit hunting apprenticeship, it seems that time in the dreamtime runs like time in dreams (Which was nowhere near as redundant a phrase in my head as it is now that I've typed it), namely, however it damn well pleases without reference to the real world. So I'm choosing to think that not-Nikolai is in fact a Foundation agent. He is suffering from the consequences of something that hasn't happened yet from the point of view of our continuity. Maybe it's an ill-advised cross-test, maybe it's a containment breach, but something is going to hit 1449 like a Japanese "research" ship and Agent maybe-John is going to get caught in the death throes.

And once he's in there, he will always have been in there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My reading of that is not that they have god-like powers of alteration, not 343 of dreamland. Just that they can do things like it was the real world and the consequences will persist within that particular "storyline".

So if you killed a person in continuity #3, he would still be dead every time anyone went back to #3; and the victim's family would still be after you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good and surreal. It touches the "native spiritual side" without becoming cliche. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, though I'm not sure about that final addendum. I really want an exploration log. Also SPC punchin' this mutha down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The way Nikolai's account degenerates into madness brings to mind this Sluggy Freelance strip, but that's not your fault.

Interesting concept. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shark? Dreamworlds? I applaud you sir. I will clap my hands like a bloody circus seal. I regret that I have but one up vote to give to this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hm, interesting from beginning to end.

However, I have a problem with this "continuity" aspect of the article. Isn't it too maleable for dreamers to use for its advantage? For example, one dreamer could change continuity to portray himself as some sort of god and force other dreamers to follow that command, until some other changes continuity again so that it never happened…? See, it's confusing…or maybe I'm the only one confused here.

Is this part of the article too exploitable for either subjects of even Agents? I find that more scary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A while back, I contemplated trying to do a Dreamtime SCP, but felt that I couldn't do it justice without offensively mangling the source material. I'm glad that you managed to pull it off better than I could've.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I let out a resounding "What?!" after reading this. Not entirely too sure what it is, but I think I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He didn't kill it himself? What the hell sort of Selachiosk Pungix Combin operative is this guy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very, very nice. Glad I could help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ugh. Well, Nikolai's crazy mind was a thing that happened inmediately after he tried to log with the dead shark or it was gradually? That could tell me more about Nikolai and his psyche before going nuts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
OH MY GOD IT IS A DREAMTIME SCP.
/OR MOSTLY DREAMTIME SCP.
//Squee.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Me likey.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Deliberate, same with all the other not-quite-dreamtime stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this. Minor point: given it appears to be based on Australian Aboriginal dreamtime mythos is the rabbit hunter archetype a deliberate or accidental anachronism? Given their recent introduction I don't know if they feature in said myths.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will cut you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They killed the dream.
With pattering, splashing feet
They danced in its corpse.
The Center rose
High and white to the sky,
Where madness never sleeps.
Under sun and moon,
Above wave and trench
The mad coats gambol about
With a whoop and a shout
On the mount where all the dreams died.

For that was the way it always had been.

Come at me bro do you even lift.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty close. The shark from Nikolai's universe is dead, though he did not kill it himself. His entry to the dreamtime is either through the shark's corpse or some other means.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-1329

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dreams give me feelings.

Especially like this.

Djoric I will cut you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The aquarium? I think I didn't read that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really well executed, and I loved the concept. Also, I feel curious if that guy was using another kind of shark to "log in" into this reality. In my mind, he killed his shark and somehow it fucked up the way he entered to this world (and also affecting his mind in the procces).+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
feels reminiscent of the aquarium

Aye, it should. Astute readers will see why.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was really pleased that you didn't make this ott and silly. It really feels reminiscent of the aquarium and Antarctica empire. Silly in concept, but done absolutely serious in execution. Very nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I looked over this in chat, loved it, and I would just like to say: Djoric, you're a bastard. A magnificent bastard, but still a bastard. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice world-building in a very short space, well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Dream Time is a little explored area of folktale on the site, and that's a shame. Good to see it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Let's try this again.

As usual, this has a whole lot of little interconnected details. See if you can pick them out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Muuuuch better than the last version. Upvoted.
